meta.title: Homelab — Architecture & Flows
meta.subtitle: Every host, container, mount and external service across rpi, opti, noblenumbat and android. Verified against a live pull of the homelab agent reports (hardware/software/network/doctor — 4/4 hosts). Pick a flow on the right to trace the path through the system, or hit Sync to refresh host status from the latest reports.

nodes.label: homelab agents
nodes.sublabel: hardware · software · network · doctor · journald-hunter · persistence-auditor · leetify

nodes.label: docs-generator (WIP)
nodes.sublabel: regen homelab/agentic/runbooks/*.md from latest reports — button exists, NOT in the dispatcher allowlist yet

nodes.label: VS Code Server
nodes.sublabel: Remote-SSH · primary ptm4 repo at ~/code/ptm4 — this page was built here

edges.label: git push (main) — primary repo copy lives here
SSH health check (HL_HOSTS now includes android — 4/4 hosts)
regenerated runbooks sync to phone (~2 min), once wired up
rsync push (exits 23 by design on the NTFS branch — verify by size, not exit code)

flows.name: rpi webapp deploy (CI/CD)
flows.description: Developer edits on noblenumbat (the primary repo copy) and pushes; the rpi's self-hosted runner rolls it out.

steps.title: You → VS Code Server (noblenumbat)
steps.detail: Remote-SSH into noblenumbat:~/code/ptm4 — the primary copy of the repo; the opti mount is stale, don't edit there

steps.title: VS Code Server → GitHub
steps.detail: git push to main — modifies homelab/RPI-srv/**

steps.title: GitHub → rpi runner
steps.detail: rpi-deploy.yml triggers; self-hosted runner on rpi polls GitHub over HTTPS (no inbound ports needed)

steps.title: rpi runner → webapp backend
steps.detail: cp compose file → /srv/docker/compose/, docker compose pull, docker compose up -d --remove-orphans

steps.title: rpi runner → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: docker compose logs --tail=200 written to /mnt/opti-fs/ptm/logging/deploy-<timestamp>.log

flows.name: Homelab agent sweep
flows.description: Scheduled + on-demand agents gather hardware/software/network/security data from every host — now 4/4 including android.

steps.title: GitHub → opti runner
steps.detail: homelab-agents.yml, runs-on: [self-hosted, opti]; homelab-doctor+network every 30 min, hardware/software/security daily

steps.title: opti runner → agents
steps.detail: agents SSH into opti/rpi/noblenumbat/android (HL_HOSTS + HL_SSH_KEY) — an unreachable host degrades gracefully, doesn't crash. Live-verified: 4/4 hosts reported on the most recent run.

steps.title: agents → android
steps.detail: android joined the monitored fleet — hardware/software/network agents now SSH in like the other three hosts

steps.title: dispatcher → agents
steps.detail: hl-agent-dispatcher.service (:9099) also runs agents on demand from an allowlist, driven by webapp Enable/Disable + Run now buttons

steps.title: webapp backend → dispatcher
steps.detail: LAN proxy call, token-authed via HL_DISPATCH_TOKEN

steps.title: agents → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: reports land in security-reports/ and agent-logs/ on opti's pool

steps.title: mergerfs → Samba
steps.detail: OMV exposes the pool as \\opti\fs

steps.title: Samba → webapp backend
steps.detail: rpi mounts the whole share at /mnt/opti-fs (CIFS, read-only for reports)

steps.title: webapp backend → nginx-webapp
steps.detail: served at https://webapp.rpi.lan:8443 — #agents and #security tabs, and this Architecture page's Sync button reads the same files

flows.name: Media file-drop pipeline
flows.description: Drop a video or .torrent file on the network share and it ends up playable in Jellyfin.

steps.title: Windows PC → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: drop video or .torrent files to \\opti.lan\fs\ptm\Media-Import\

steps.title: mergerfs → media-import.timer
steps.detail: noblenumbat mounts the inbox at /mnt/opti-media; systemd timer scans it every 2 min

steps.title: media-import → arr stack
steps.detail: stable video files move to /srv/media/staging/ and trigger Radarr: DownloadedMoviesScan

steps.title: media-import → qBittorrent
steps.detail: .torrent files move to /srv/media/blackhole/, a qBittorrent watch folder

steps.title: qBittorrent → gluetun
steps.detail: qBittorrent runs inside gluetun's network namespace — all torrent traffic is VPN-routed

steps.title: gluetun → ProtonVPN
steps.detail: OpenVPN tunnel, free tier, Netherlands servers only, no port forwarding

steps.title: arr stack → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: Radarr renames + imports into the movie library on opti (cross-device copy, no hardlinks)

steps.title: Jellyfin → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: realtime monitor picks up the new file from /mnt/opti-library automatically

steps.title: Browser → Jellyfin
steps.detail: stream the newly imported title, hardware-transcoded via Intel QuickSync VAAPI

flows.name: LAN DNS resolution
flows.description: Every device on the LAN resolves names through Pi-hole first.

steps.title: Browser → Pi-hole
steps.detail: all LAN DNS hits 192.168.1.10:53 — local records (bitwarden.rpi.lan, webapp.rpi.lan, jellyfin.lan, comics.lan, opti.lan, android.lan) resolve directly

steps.title: Pi-hole → upstream DNS
steps.detail: anything not blocked or locally defined forwards to the router (192.168.1.1) with 1.1.1.1 as fallback

flows.name: Vaultwarden login
flows.description: Password manager traffic, TLS-terminated on rpi.

steps.title: Browser → nginx-bitwarden
steps.detail: HTTPS :443, self-signed cert for bitwarden.rpi.lan

steps.title: nginx-bitwarden → Vaultwarden
steps.detail: proxy_pass to the internal bridge network, port 80 (no direct host exposure)

steps.title: Vaultwarden → MariaDB
steps.detail: mysql://bitwarden@bitwarden-db:3306/bitwarden — stop_grace_period 60s to avoid tc.log corruption

flows.name: Discord bot notification
flows.description: Health-digest bot posts a daily summary pulled from agent reports.

steps.title: discord-healthdigest → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: reads agent-logs/ (CIFS /mnt/opti-fs) plus Pi-hole + dispatcher status for the digest

steps.title: discord-* bots → Discord
steps.detail: each bot posts to its own webhook URL (weather, healthdigest, jellyfin, sports, hltv)

flows.name: Comics: Mylar3 → Kavita → Mihon
flows.description: Automated comic acquisition, then offline reading on the phone.

steps.title: Mylar3 → ComicVine
steps.detail: free API key for series/issue metadata

steps.title: Mylar3 → qBittorrent
steps.detail: sends matched torrents to qBittorrent (label/category: comics)

steps.title: qBittorrent → gluetun
steps.detail: download still routes through the VPN like everything else

steps.title: Phone → Mylar3/Kavita
steps.detail: Kavita serves completed comics; Mihon (Kavita extension) syncs chapter-level read progress over OPDS — no VPN, LAN-only by design

flows.name: Indexer search (Prowlarr + FlareSolverr)
flows.description: Cloudflare-protected torrent/usenet sites, bypassed for automated search.

steps.title: Prowlarr → indexers
steps.detail: requests route through the FlareSolverr proxy (:8191) to solve Cloudflare challenges before hitting tagged indexers

flows.name: Android: fleet monitoring + backup
flows.description: The Galaxy S10 was brought into the monitored fleet and backs its data up to opti.

steps.title: You → android
steps.detail: ssh android (Termux, port 8022, user u0_a204, key ~/.ssh/homelab) — unrooted, so privileged commands need adb, not sudo

steps.title: agents → android
steps.detail: hardware/software/network agents SSH in on the same schedule as the other three hosts; homelab-doctor currently flags the root disk at 97% full

steps.title: rsync backup → mergerfs pool
steps.detail: ~14 GB (installers/media/docs) pushed to /srv/pool/ptm/android/; exit code 23 is expected on the pool's NTFS branch (no POSIX utime) — verify success by comparing sizes, not the exit status

flows.name: Local LLM assistant (android)
flows.description: Private, offline inference on the phone — no cloud API cost. Surfaced in the webapp's Local LLM tab.

steps.title: webapp backend → llama-ctl
steps.detail: management API (:8081) — status, model list/switch, runbook CRUD, and grounded /ask (server-side stuffs the runbooks as context)

steps.title: webapp backend → llama-server
steps.detail: raw inference (:8080) — POST /v1/chat/completions, OpenAI-compatible; qwen2.5 3B (default, better judgment) or 1.5B (fast path), CPU-only, one model resident at a time

steps.title: webapp backend → docs-generator
steps.detail: the 'Regenerate docs' button POSTs /api/agents/docs-generator/run — as of this scan that agent name isn't in agent-dispatcher.py's AGENTS allowlist yet, so clicking it will currently fail until that's wired up

steps.title: docs-generator → llama-ctl
steps.detail: once wired up: regenerates homelab/agentic/runbooks/*.md from the latest agent reports, syncs to the phone within ~2 minutes for grounded Q&A
